name: Naturepedia Plant Soil Microbial Knowledge Mesh
description: Paid relationship mesh connecting plant intelligence, soil systems, microbial life, mycorrhizal networks, nutrient cycling, carbon storage, water movement, and regenerative feedback pathways.
architecture: Plant Intelligence → Roots → Soil Systems → Soil Microbiome → Mycorrhizal Networks → Microbial Life → Nutrient Cycling → Carbon Storage → Water Systems → Regenerative Feedbacks

relationshipMesh.relationship: Plants sense water, nutrients, gravity, touch, light, microbes, fungi, stress, and neighboring plants through root systems.
Roots structure soil, feed microbial life, stabilize aggregates, move carbon belowground, and create exchange interfaces.
Soil systems host bacteria, archaea, fungi, biofilms, decomposers, nutrient cyclers, and living food-web communities.
Microbes and fungi form underground exchange networks connecting roots, minerals, water, carbon compounds, and ecological memory.
Mycorrhizal networks support chemical signaling, resource sharing, warning cues, and soil-network feedback.
Microbes transform organic matter, minerals, nitrogen, phosphorus, carbon compounds, and chemical energy into plant-available pathways.
Nutrient cycling regulates decomposition, photosynthesis, root exudates, microbial biomass, soil organic matter, and carbon storage.
Water infiltration, retention, groundwater recharge, drought buffering, and soil structure determine biological activity.
Regenerative soil pathways strengthen feedback among plants, microbes, fungi, water, carbon, biodiversity, and resilience.

protectedRetrievalUse: plant soil microbial knowledge mesh retrieval
